analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-494

POL-494.title: IMPLEMENT: Provider Routing Evidence
POL-494.body:
## Objective

Make provider routing decisions explainable in run evidence and closeout artifacts without redesigning routing behavior.

## Context

Created from [POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence). Current evidence shows recent Polaris runs can select the same provider for every child while the operator sees only a compact provider field and a one-provider `providers_tried` list. Current code uses legacy compatibility mode when `execution.routerPolicy.providerRegistry` is absent, while the Worker Router spec expects candidate evidence when registry metadata exists. This cluster preserves existing routing semantics and adds enough evidence, reporting, anomaly referral, and retention behavior for PR/run review.

## Goal

Provide an implementation-ready surface that explains provider selection, clarifies role policy order, reports routing evidence at closeout, flags routing anomalies for Medic/SOL review, and retains relevant telemetry with finalized run archives.

## Scope

* smartdocs/raw/analysis/**
* smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md
* docs/USAGE.md
* docs/SETUP.md
* src/config/**
* src/loop/dispatch.ts
* src/loop/dispatch-state.ts
* src/loop/router/**
* src/loop/adapters/**
* src/finalize/**
* src/autoresearch/**
* src/qc/**
* src/medic/**
* .polaris/clusters/POL-*/clusters.json

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Current provider routing behavior is documented from [POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence) evidence.
- [ ] Role policy ordering semantics are explicit in docs/config comments and validation where appropriate.
- [ ] Runtime telemetry exposes a compact routing evidence shape for compatibility and router modes.
- [ ] Final run reports include provider/routing evidence useful for PR review.
- [ ] Routing anomalies have clear Medic/SOL referral triggers.
- [ ] Telemetry/run artifact retention rules are documented and implemented where minimal.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop src/finalize src/autoresearch src/qc src/config

## Ordering

* Execute children in listed order. Evidence schema and policy semantics come before closeout rendering; anomaly and retention work can follow after evidence is stable.

## Non-goals

* Do not redesign the Worker Router.
* Do not change default provider choice or enable cross-provider fallback by default.
* Do not silently mutate provider policy, trust scores, or runtime routing thresholds.
* Do not dispatch implementation work from this analysis session.

POL-543.title: Add polaris qc resolve command and wire finalize gate to accept it

POL-542.title: IMPLEMENT: QC Repair-Loop Operator Resolution Path

POL-544.title: Address QC findings: status-defaulting hardening and telemetry path DRY
POL-544.body:
## Objective

Apply two minor, genuine coderabbit QC findings from [POL-494](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-494/implement-provider-routing-evidence)'s round-2 QC pass that were routed to `operator-review` (unclear attribution) but confirmed as valid, low-risk improvements.

## Context

During [POL-494](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-494/implement-provider-routing-evidence) finalize, QC round 2 (`coderabbit-1783818427444`) flagged 7 items; most were false positives against untracked runtime scratch, but two are genuine:

1. `src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts` **(around** `normalizeLegacyCompactReturn`**, lines \~630-643)**: missing or unrecognized `status` values currently default to `"done"` rather than `"failed"`. This means `buildCompactReturnForSealedResult` can treat a zero-exit result with an ambiguous/unrecognized status as a clean success. Fix: default unrecognized/missing status to `"failed"`, while preserving existing mappings for recognized legacy synonyms (`"success"`/`"completed"` → `"done"`) and already-valid statuses (`"done"`/`"failed"`/`"blocked"`).
2. `src/finalize/steps/05-generate-report.ts` **(lines \~15-19)**: telemetry file path is constructed with duplicated conditional join logic instead of calling the existing `resolveTelemetryFilePath(state, repoRoot)` helper. Fix: call the helper and pass its result to `readJsonLines`, removing the duplicated `artifact_dir` fallback logic.

## Goal

Both findings applied with minimal, targeted changes — no unrelated refactoring.

## Scope

* src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts
* src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.test.ts
* src/finalize/steps/05-generate-report.ts
* src/finalize/steps/05-generate-report.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `normalizeLegacyCompactReturn` defaults missing/unrecognized `status` to `"failed"`, not `"done"`.
- [ ] Recognized legacy synonyms (`"success"`, `"completed"` → `"done"`) and already-valid statuses (`"done"`, `"failed"`, `"blocked"`) are unchanged.
- [ ] A zero-exit worker result with an ambiguous/unrecognized status is no longer treated as a clean success.
- [ ] `05-generate-report.ts` calls `resolveTelemetryFilePath(state, repoRoot)` instead of duplicating path-join logic; duplicated fallback removed.
- [ ] Regression tests added for both: an unrecognized-status CompactReturn defaults to failed; report generation uses the shared telemetry path resolver.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.test.ts src/finalize/steps/05-generate-report.test.ts

## Ordering

* None — independent of other [POL-494](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-494/implement-provider-routing-evidence) children (all already done).

## Non-goals

* Do not touch the other QC round-2 findings — those are confirmed false positives against untracked runtime scratch files and will be resolved via `polaris qc resolve`, not code changes.
* Do not change the CompactReturn schema itself.
* Do not refactor unrelated parts of either file.

POL-541.title: extractSummary must not accept non-object JSON primitives as CompactReturn

POL-540.title: Fix CompactReturn child_id validation order in terminal-cli adapter
POL-540.body:
## Objective

Fix a false-failure bug in `terminal-cli` worker result handling: a worker that completed successfully (commit made, validation passed) is marked as `status: "failure"` because its CompactReturn JSON omitted `child_id`, even though the runtime already knows the correct value.

## Context

Discovered during a [POL-494](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-494/implement-provider-routing-evidence) run: [POL-497](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-497/add-routing-evidence-summary-to-run-reports-and-closeout) was dispatched to the `devin` provider (routed through the shared `terminal-cli` adapter — there is no dedicated devin adapter). The worker made a real commit (`e284a3c`) and reported `validation: "passed"`, but its stdout CompactReturn omitted `child_id`. The runtime's `polaris loop run` halted with `worker-error` and the sealed result recorded `status: "failure"`, `error_message: "CompactReturn validation failed: missing or empty child_id"` — despite the work being genuinely done.

Root cause is in `writeSealedResultIfNeeded()` in `src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts`:

```ts
const normalized = normalizeLegacyCompactReturn(parsed);
const compactReturnErrors = validateCompactReturn(normalized);   // validates BEFORE fallback
const isValidCompactReturn = compactReturnErrors.length === 0;
const effectiveStatus = (exitCode === 0 && isValidCompactReturn) ? "success" : "failure";

const sealedResult = {
  run_id: packet.run_id,
  child_id: String(normalized["child_id"] ?? packet.active_child),  // fallback applied AFTER validation
  ...
```

`sealedResult.child_id` already falls back to `packet.active_child` when the worker's raw JSON omits it, but `validateCompactReturn(normalized)` runs against the raw object before that fallback is applied, so validation fails and `effectiveStatus` is forced to `"failure"` even though the sealed result ends up with a correct `child_id`.

`normalizeLegacyCompactReturn()` already backfills `tracker_updated`/`state_updated`/`telemetry_updated` when missing — this is the natural place to also backfill `child_id`.

## Goal

A worker's CompactReturn that omits `child_id` (but is otherwise valid, with the runtime already knowing the correct child from `packet.active_child`) should not be misclassified as a failure.

## Scope

* src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts
* src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `normalizeLegacyCompactReturn` (or an equivalent fix point) backfills `child_id` from `packet.active_child` when the raw worker JSON omits or empties it, before `validateCompactReturn` runs.
- [ ] A worker CompactReturn missing `child_id` but otherwise valid, with `exit_code === 0`, produces `effectiveStatus: "success"` and a correctly populated `sealedResult.child_id`.
- [ ] Existing validation behavior for CompactReturns with a genuinely wrong/malformed `child_id` (e.g., mismatched from `packet.active_child`) is unchanged — only the missing/empty case is backfilled.
- [ ] Regression test added reproducing the [POL-497](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-497/add-routing-evidence-summary-to-run-reports-and-closeout) scenario: exit_code 0, commit present, validation "passed", `child_id` omitted from raw stdout JSON.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.test.ts

## Non-goals

* Do not change the CompactReturn schema itself (`src/loop/compact-return.ts`).
* Do not add provider-specific (devin-only) handling — the fix must be provider-agnostic since terminal-cli is shared by all CLI-based providers.
* Do not retroactively repair the [POL-497](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-497/add-routing-evidence-summary-to-run-reports-and-closeout) sealed result file — that is a one-off artifact, not something this child should touch.

POL-499.title: Define retention and archive rules for routing telemetry evidence
POL-499.body:
## Objective

Ensure routing telemetry and run evidence needed for review survives finalize/archive with clear retention rules.

## Context

[POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence) asks for retention rules for old telemetry and run artifacts. Current finalize archive copies `current-state.json`, `run-report.md`, and map files into `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/`, while raw taskchain telemetry remains under `.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/runs/<run-id>/telemetry.jsonl`, which artifact policy treats as workspace scratch. That leaves PR reviewers with less durable routing evidence than the analysis requires.

## Goal

Promote the minimal routing evidence needed for audit/review while keeping noisy workspace scratch out of delivery commits.

## Scope

* src/finalize/steps/12-archive.ts
* src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts
* src/finalize/artifact-policy.test.ts
* src/finalize/steps/POLARIS.md
* docs/USAGE.md
* smartdocs/architecture/runtime-state-consolidation-spec.md
* smartdocs/architecture/polaris-run-ledger.md
* .gitignore if a pattern update is required

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Finalize archives raw telemetry or a compact routing evidence extract into `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/`.
- [ ] Artifact promotion policy distinguishes durable archived run evidence from workspace scratch telemetry.
- [ ] Documentation states what is retained indefinitely, what is workspace scratch, and what may be pruned after archive.
- [ ] Retention docs cover `.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/runs/<run-id>/telemetry.jsonl`, `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/run-report.md`, `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/current-state.json`, `.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl`, and cluster packet/result artifacts.
- [ ] Tests verify archive behavior and artifact policy classification.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-497](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-497/add-routing-evidence-summary-to-run-reports-and-closeout) if the closeout routing summary writes a separate evidence extract. Otherwise it can run after [POL-496](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-496/emit-compact-provider-routing-evidence-for-dispatch-review).

## Non-goals

* Do not delete old telemetry in this child unless a clearly reversible prune command already exists.
* Do not commit `.taskchain_artifacts/**`.
* Do not remove `.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl` or active cluster evidence.

POL-498.title: Route provider routing anomalies to Medic and SOL review
POL-498.body:
## Objective

Define and implement minimal provider routing anomaly triggers that feed Medic/SOL review surfaces without automatically changing routing behavior.

## Context

[POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence) asks when run anomalies should trigger Medic review. Current SOL scoring already summarizes router outcome events and QC repair routing exists, but recent [POL-470](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-470/implement-polaris-quality-control-lifecycle) evidence shows useful anomalies that are not clearly summarized: repeated same-provider selection despite multi-provider policy, stale dispatch aborts, missing sealed result files, invalid inline attempts, and provider/fallback evidence gaps.

## Goal

Treat routing anomalies as review signals that can produce diagnosis hints or follow-up proposals, while keeping routing changes review-gated.

## Scope

* src/autoresearch/score.ts
* src/autoresearch/score.test.ts
* src/autoresearch/proposal.ts
* src/autoresearch/proposal.test.ts
* src/qc/routing.ts
* src/qc/orchestration.ts
* src/medic/**
* src/types/result-packet.ts
* smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md
* smartdocs/medic/**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SOL/router outcome summary detects repeated same-provider selection when policy evidence says multiple providers were eligible or configured.
- [ ] SOL/router outcome summary detects evidence gaps separately from true provider failures.
- [ ] Missing sealed result, stale dispatch abort, and invalid inline attempt telemetry are classified as Medic/state-repair review signals.
- [ ] Proposal generation maps recurring routing anomalies to review-gated follow-up issues, not automatic config mutation.
- [ ] Tests cover provider monopoly, missing evidence, provider exhaustion, and state-repair anomaly cases.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/qc src/medic

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-496](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-496/emit-compact-provider-routing-evidence-for-dispatch-review) for the normalized dispatch evidence fields. Can run before or after [POL-497](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-497/add-routing-evidence-summary-to-run-reports-and-closeout) if the evidence shape is stable.

## Non-goals

* Do not auto-run Medic from SOL scoring without explicit runtime support.
* Do not change provider trust or policy automatically.
* Do not mark a successful run failed solely because routing evidence is incomplete.

POL-497.title: Add routing evidence summary to run reports and closeout
POL-497.body:
## Objective

Include a compact provider routing evidence summary in finalized run reports and closeout artifacts.

## Context

[POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence) asks for routing evidence that reviewers can inspect in PR and run closeout. Current `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/run-report.md` lists children and validation but not provider distribution, policy reason, candidate/fallback evidence, registry mode, or anomalies. Recent [POL-470](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-470/implement-polaris-quality-control-lifecycle) run reports therefore do not explain why every child used the same provider.

## Goal

Render routing evidence from telemetry and state into final run reports without requiring reviewers to manually inspect JSONL telemetry.

## Scope

* src/finalize/run-report.ts
* src/finalize/run-report.test.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/steps/05-generate-report.ts
* src/loop/finalize-evidence.ts
* src/autoresearch/score.ts
* .polaris/runs/**/run-report.md fixtures if tests use fixtures

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Run reports summarize provider distribution by child.
- [ ] Run reports include selected provider, selection reason, compatibility/router mode, provider policy order, and fallback/candidate counts when available.
- [ ] Run reports call out missing registry metadata when multiple worker providers are configured but candidate evidence is absent.
- [ ] Run reports call out routing anomalies such as repeated same-provider selection, provider exhaustion, fallback attempts, stale dispatch aborts, and missing sealed results.
- [ ] Tests cover a run with all children on one provider and a run with full router candidate evidence.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize src/autoresearch src/loop/finalize-evidence.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-496](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-496/emit-compact-provider-routing-evidence-for-dispatch-review) because closeout should consume the normalized evidence shape.

## Non-goals

* Do not change finalize delivery gates except to report evidence.
* Do not block PR creation solely because all children used one provider.
* Do not require raw telemetry inspection for normal review.

POL-496.title: Emit compact provider routing evidence for dispatch review
POL-496.body:
## Objective

Normalize provider routing evidence emitted during dispatch so PR and run reviewers can see why a provider was selected and which alternatives were considered.

## Context

[POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence) evidence from [POL-470](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-470/implement-polaris-quality-control-lifecycle) shows `child-dispatched` and `child-complete` events recording provider `devin`, `router_selection_reason: role-policy`, and `providers_tried: [devin]` for every child. `provider-selected` telemetry is only useful when router evidence is present, and compatibility mode currently records too little context for multi-provider policy review.

## Goal

Add a compact, stable routing evidence shape that works for both compatibility mode and router mode without changing selection behavior.

## Scope

* src/loop/dispatch.ts
* src/loop/dispatch-state.ts
* src/loop/checkpoint.ts
* src/loop/router/**
* src/loop/adapters/**
* src/loop/*.test.ts
* src/loop/adapters/**tests**/**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Dispatch telemetry records effective policy order, selected provider, selection reason, compatibility mode, registry presence, and fallback eligibility.
- [ ] Compatibility mode records enough evidence to explain why only one provider was selected while preserving legacy behavior.
- [ ] Router mode continues to include candidate eligibility, rejection reasons, and scores.
- [ ] `child-dispatched` and `child-complete` carry consistent provider/routing summary fields.
- [ ] Tests cover multi-provider policy with missing registry metadata and router mode with registry metadata.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-495](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-495/document-provider-routing-behavior-and-policy-semantics) terminology and current-behavior documentation.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement new provider ranking rules.
* Do not enable post-start retries.
* Do not change the selected provider for existing compatibility-mode configs.

POL-495.title: Document provider routing behavior and policy semantics
POL-495.body:
## Objective

Document the current provider routing behavior behind [POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence), including compatibility mode, provider registry requirements, and role policy ordering semantics.

## Context

[POL-493](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-493/analyze-provider-routing-evidence) found that current config can list multiple worker providers while current run evidence only shows one tried provider. The key code path is `resolveProviderAndMode`: when `execution.routerPolicy.providerRegistry` is absent, it bypasses the new router engine and uses legacy role-policy selection. The Worker Router spec already describes provider ranking and fallback but needs a concrete current-behavior note for operators.

## Goal

Make it clear whether `providerPolicy.<role>.providers` is priority, fallback order, or eligibility-only under both compatibility mode and router mode.

## Scope

* smartdocs/raw/analysis/**
* smartdocs/specs/active/worker-router-architecture.md
* docs/USAGE.md
* docs/SETUP.md
* src/config/schema.ts
* src/config/schema.json
* src/config/validator.ts
* src/config/validator.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Docs explicitly state that role policy order is the provider preference/fallback order unless rotation is configured.
- [ ] Docs explain that an empty or missing `execution.routerPolicy.providerRegistry` puts dispatch in compatibility mode.
- [ ] Docs explain why compatibility evidence may show only the selected provider in `providers_tried`.
- [ ] Config comments/schema descriptions do not imply multiple providers are tried when registry metadata is absent.
- [ ] Validation warns or tests cover ambiguous multi-provider policy without registry metadata, if a warning surface already exists.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/config

## Ordering

* None. This child should run first because later evidence/report changes should use the clarified terminology.

## Non-goals

* Do not change provider selection behavior.
* Do not require every repo to define `execution.routerPolicy.providerRegistry`.
* Do not remove compatibility mode.
